metadata.description: The naming prefix for all resources to be deployed.
The optional middle part naming for all resources to be deployment. Suggested as the business unit, project or domain.
The environment name abbreviation used for the deployment.
The Azure region where all resources will be deployed.
Optional naming abbreviation for the Azure Data Lake Storage account.
Optional naming abbreviation for the Azure Storage account used to support the Azure Functions App.
The numeric identifier for all resources as a naming suffix, used to differentiate between instances of resources.
The product SKU for the Azure Databricks workspace.
The product SKU for the App Service Plan used by the Azure Function App.
Deploy the SQL DACPAC required for the metadata database objects.
An external IP address that is allowed access to the Azure SQL Database, as part of the logical SQL instance Firewall Rules.
Allow Azure services to access the Azure SQL Database, as part of the logical SQL instance Firewall Rules. Required for Azure Data Factory MI authentication.
A timestamp format used for the deployment execution naming only. Used to differentiate between instances of resources deployed.
Optionally deploy Azure Data Factory, if it already exists.
Optionally deploy a separate Azure Data Factory instance to house Worker and Bootstrap pipelines separately.
Optionally deploy an Azure SQL Database to house all metadata, if it already exists.
Optionally deploy an Azure Function App, if it already exists.
Optionally deploy an Azure Databricks workspace, if it already exists.
Optionally setup role assignments as part of the deployment.
Optionally deploy a custom VNet for the Azure Databricks workspace.
Optionally deploy a Virtual Machine to house self-hosted Integration Runtime (IR) for Azure Data Factory.
Optionally configure GitHub repository for Azure Data Factory.

appSettings.name: AzureWebJobsStorage
appSettings.value: [format('DefaultEndpointsProtocol=https;AccountName={0};AccountKey={1};BlobEndpoint=https://{2}.blob.core.windows.net/;FileEndpoint=https://{3}.file.core.windows.net/;TableEndpoint=https://{4}.table.core.windows.net/;QueueEndpoint=https://{5}.queue.core.windows.net/', variables('storageAccountName'), listKeys(resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', variables('storageAccountName')), '2023-05-01').keys[0].value, variables('storageAccountName'), variables('storageAccountName'), variables('storageAccountName'), variables('storageAccountName'))]

metadata.description: Resource group location.
Resource name prefix as per template naming concatenated in the main file.
Resource name suffix as per template naming concatenated in the main file.
Add firewall rule for user's local IP Address.
Add firewall rule for Azure Resources.
Whether to deploy the DACPAC
Random GUID used to create a SQL Server admin password.

properties.scriptContent:
$dacpacPath = "$env:TEMP\db.dacpac"
      Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "${dacpacSasUrl}" -OutFile $dacpacPath
      Install-Module -Name SqlServer -Force -Scope CurrentUser
      $securePassword = ConvertTo-SecureString "${sqlPassword}" -AsPlainText -Force
      $creds = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential ("sqladmin", $securePassword)
      Publish-DacPac -ServerName "${serverName}.database.windows.net" -DatabaseName "${databaseName}" -DacPacPath $dacpacPath -SqlCredential $creds -AuthenticationType SqlPassword
